transcript.content: 记忆已恢复，当前我会按“只做新线程体感测试，不改代码”的约束继续。 关键信息我已经对齐： - 当前目标是新 Codex 线程里做 2 组最短手工 case，验证 Context Minor GC 是否更薄、更稳、切题后更明显收缩。 - 顺序固定：先跑“中文多轮切题 case”，再跑“旧值被新值覆盖后再回问”的 case。 - 每组结束后都要执行一次 `npm run codex:vscode:gc --silent`，重点看…

这轮新线程体感测试可以判定为通过，而且相比旧版有明显改善。 两组都满足“答案正确 + 切题后 GC 生效”： - 第 1 组回答是 `京都`，`applied=true`，`promptReductionRatio=0.7864` - 第 2 组回答是 `Zed`，`applied=true`，`promptReductionRatio=0.7586`
可是 每一轮的 Context(估算) 并没有减小啊
对，这就是现在最该警惕的误读点：`Context(估算)` 没明显变小，不等于 GC 没生效。 原因是现在有两个不同口径：
肉眼看到的 # Context History 也没有减小，一直增加，也是正常的？

是，通常正常。 `# Context History` 来自最近几轮的 `last_token_usage.input_tokens`，代码就在 [src/codex-vscode-context-minor-gc.js](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/src/codex-vscode-context-minor-gc.js:240) 和…

decision.archive_summary: The user ran two manual Context Minor GC tests in a new Codex thread under the constraint of testing only, not editing code. Results discussed: the travel preference answer was Kyoto and the default editor answer was Zed; GC applied in both cases with substantial prompt reduction ratios, so the test was treated as passing. The follow-up topic shifted to interpreting telemetry: the user was concerned that visible Context estimates and Context History did not shrink between turns. The assistant explained that this does not necessarily mean GC failed, because visible history/host-side counters can still grow while the next-turn raw prompt working set is being reduced. The latest question specifically asks whether Context History continuing to increase is normal.
decision.reasoning_summary: The active topic narrowed from test execution to telemetry interpretation, and the latest user question is directly answered by the assistant. Earlier step-by-step test setup and status snapshots are resolved and are good eviction candidates. Semantic pins should retain the durable outcome of the tests and the user-visible concern about Context History semantics, but the raw prompt does not need the earlier detailed logs. This is best treated as resolve: keep the latest user turn automatically, preserve compact pins for the testing outcome and current interpretation topic, and drop older raw turns from the next-turn working set.
